title: Nigeria
body: Nigeria is high impact and high feasibility because coalition infrastructure exists and the bill already has visible legislative momentum.
action: Push Senate passage of HB.244 and define implementation architecture before enactment.

title: Kenya
body: Kenya is framed as a strong implementation case where binding guidelines exist but consolidation into primary legislation remains unfinished.
action: Translate guideline strength into consolidated primary legislation with child-specific statutory duties.

title: South Africa
body: South Africa is positioned as a high-value target for consolidation because multiple protections already exist across fragmented laws.
action: Consolidate POPIA, Cybercrimes Act, and child-protection provisions into a dedicated COP framework.

title: DPA engagement
body: Functioning data protection authorities can serve as practical rule-setting entry points while broader law develops.
action: Use active DPAs to issue child-data directives while broader legislative reform is still pending.

title: Rwanda
body: Rwanda sits in the high-impact and medium-feasibility tier because ministerial and planning infrastructure already exists.
action: Move from ministerial order and action plan into primary legislation with rights-respecting design controls.

title: Malawi
body: Malawi needs support for MACRA and a shift from strategy work into associated regulations and stronger legal backing.
action: Back MACRA’s strategy work with enforceable regulations and stronger legislative anchoring.

title: Ethiopia
body: Ethiopia is framed as a strategic medium-feasibility target, with the February 2026 CRC recommendations providing leverage.
action: Use the February 2026 CRC recommendations to open a dedicated child online safety reform pathway.

title: ECOWAS
body: An ECOWAS directive would support sub-regional harmonisation and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
action: Develop an ECOWAS-level directive to reduce fragmentation and regulatory arbitrage.

title: Zambia
body: Zambia is identified as an implementation-stage jurisdiction where strategy backing should evolve into legislation.
action: Convert implementation momentum from the 2025 strategy into legislative backing.

title: AU scorecard
body: A public AU implementation scorecard is treated as a long-term structural tool for pressure, benchmarking, and visibility.
action: Publish a repeatable AU scorecard that benchmarks domestication and enforcement readiness.

title: Evidence generation
body: Broader Francophone, Lusophone, and North African evidence generation is needed to close the research gap.
action: Commission new evidence cycles beyond Eastern and Southern Africa, especially in Francophone and North African states.

title: Model legislation
body: Continental model legislation is treated as a long-term structural asset for consistency and rights-respecting design.
action: Draft model legislation that standardizes child-safety duties while preserving rights safeguards.

title: Rights-impact reviews
body: Rights-impact assessments of recent cyber laws are needed to identify misuse and overbreadth risks early.
action: Run rights-impact reviews on recent cyber laws before abusive provisions become entrenched.
